Quebec is in the 7th wave, but no COVID-19 restrictions expected: public health   The Quebec health minister said vaccination is an important tool as Quebec learns to "live with the virus."

Quebec reports 34 more monkeypox cases as global toll tops 6,000
As Alouettes struggle, GM fires head coach Khari Jones
Lawsuit alleging racism prompts calls for independent inquiry into Repentigny police
Laval police seize pistols, ammo and cash in Brossard raid   A SWAT team from the Longueuil police assisted in the operation targeting urban violence.

Bran Van 3000 in control of the party as it prepares for Montreal jazz fest show   “I think we’re all blown away by how easy and smooth it is,” ringleader James Di Salvio said of the Montreal collective picking up where it left off.

Deterrent or discrimination? Quebec defends police power to make random traffic stops   Lawyer Michel Deom told Superior Court the problem isn’t the power itself but its abuse by some police officers.
Montreal North shooting victim will be questioned by police   The sound of gunfire was reported to police around 10 p.m. Wednesday at the corner of Lapierre and Pascale Sts.

Montreal exhibit by Yayoi Kusama, 93, invites us to contemplate universe   The Japanese artist is best known for her Infinity Mirrored Rooms, in which solitary viewers are plunked into an expansive disco cosmos.
Doctors urge Quebec to strengthen its tone as COVID numbers climb
Quebec is in the 7th wave, but no COVID-19 restrictions expected: public health
Former NHLer Bryan Marchment dies suddenly during trip to draft in Montreal

Dominique Ducharme says he knew his time with Canadiens was up
Kramberger: Civic projects cost big bucks and citizens should see the price tag   Too often, public statements or press releases about welcomed projects don’t mention the associated cost.
Opinion: There's more that can be done to fight COVID   We shouldn't have to choose between lockdowns and "let it rip."

Martine St-Victor: Let's make Montreal the world's most liveable city   Montreal didn't fare so well on the Economist's 2022 Global Liveability Index. Yes, the city is still great, but how do we make it greater?

Cohen: Irving Abella — A self-effacing Jewish colossus who never left his roots   If there was one touchstone of Abella's Jewishness, it was food. What a pleasure it was to dine at his groaning table. The feast, intellectual or otherwise, was memorable.

Tom Mulcair: Horne smelter issue opens a can of worms for Legault   Situation in Rouyn-Noranda has reminded people that his government has spent four years ignoring environmental and social issues like this one.
Shea Weber left Canadiens as quietly as he arrived | HI/O Bonus   The panel discuss the fallout from the former Canadiens captain’s trade to the Vegas Golden Knights and his legacy in Montreal.

Montreal swimmer Harvey says she was drugged at world championships   Harvey said in an Instagram post that there is a four-to-six hour window where she has no recollection of what happened, and that she remembers waking up with the Canadian team manager and doctor by her bedside.

Stu Cowan: There would be a lot for Canadiens to like about Juraj Slafkovsky   The 6-foot-4, 229-pound left-winger can picture himself playing on a line with Nick Suzuki and Cole Caufield if the Habs decide to draft him.
Canadiens will pick Wright, but will he jump straight to the NHL?   The Canadiens have little choice but to go for Wright, who represents a safe, if not altogether exciting, prospect.
CF Montréal to honour former captain Mauro Biello   Fans are invited to wear old Montreal Impact jerseys for induction ceremony into the club's Wall of Fame on July 16 at Stade Saputo.
Pointe-Claire halts work in Fairview Forest after trees felled   Save Fairview Forest environmental group is angry that trees were cut to build a fence around forest.

Paper Excellence Group signs deal to buy Montreal's Resolute Forest Products   Under the proposal, the parent company of Domtar Corp. will pay US$20.50 per Resolute share. The company will also keep Resolute's headquarters in Montreal.
Montreal home sales drop 11% since last year: association   The figures are below 4,333, the monthly sales average since 2017, and signal that the market is continuing to slow.
Air Canada temporarily bans pets from baggage hold, cites delays   Air Canada’s smaller rival WestJet Airlines said it will continue accepting animals.
Côte-des-Neiges groups demand 2,500 social housing units for Blue Bonnets site   About 2,500 households are currently on a waiting list for social housing in Côte-des-Neiges, community groups say.

'Concerts feed our souls': Nathaniel Rateliff's fans wet and wild at Montreal jazz fest   Almost all dropped their umbrellas to embrace the rain — and, seemingly, the return of large-scale festivals to the city.

Photos: Montreal jazz fest blowout with Nathaniel Rateliff & the Night Sweats   Nathaniel Rateliff & the Night Sweats played a free outdoor concert on the TD Stage at the Place des Festivals on Tuesday, July 5, as part of the 42nd Montreal International Jazz Festival. Earlier in the evening, Stacey Ryan played the main stage. Montreal Gazette photographer Allen McInnis snapped these shots.

Basia Bulat's Montreal jazz fest show comes with strings attached   The Montreal singer says her new string-quartet album The Garden was a unique opportunity to revisit songs she had written years earlier.
Brownstein: Oliver Jones set to talk jazz, not play it, at art exhibition   “It’s great to see the city coming alive again during the jazz festival. Even if I’m not playing, I want to be part of it.”

Outting the lie of Go Ask Alice, the so-called diary of a troubled teen on drugs   Journalist Rick Emerson finds the teen best-seller didn't come from the diary of 'Alice.' Rather, it came from the mind of Beatrice Sparks

Six O’Clock Solution: Mix it up with sticky honey salmon   The recipe comes with variations on the vegetables — asparagus and peas can be replaced with string beans and kale — from an easygoing cookbook.

Could a Roe v. Wade-style reversal of abortion rights happen in Canada?   Prior attempts to make abortion illegal have failed — but abortion access and misinformation are still big problems in Canada.

Here are 5 cool new things to try at SÉPAQ parks this summer   Discover what the glaciers left behind. Count the stars at a Dark Sky Reserve. Or pit yourself against your siblings at a ninja-style obstacle course.
Here's how you and the kids can have a busy and happy summer vacation   The school-year routine makes our children feel secure. But when summer vacation arrives, the entire family must readapt.
Bill Zacharkiw's Wines of the Week: July 2, 2022   Every week, Bill Zacharkiw identifies his top wine picks available at the SAQ and offers ideas for food pairings.

True Crime Byline: A Politician Murdered his Ex Wife. We Should Have Seen it Coming   Looking back on the murder of JoAnn Wilson today, what's most surprising is that we were surprised it happened at all.

True Crime Byline podcast: The Article that Blew Up a Murder Trial   Listen to this podcast episode for the shocking story of how a National Post article about a teen vampire led to a mistrial, as told by reporter Joseph Brean

Douglas Todd: Canadian cops far less likely to kill, or be killed, than U.S. counterparts   Analysis: Canadian police are often accused of using excessive force, but a U.S. officer is five times more likely than a Canadian cop to shoot and kill.

True Crime Byline: Families knew 'horrible creature' was taking Vancouver women long before police did   The DNA of 33 missing women was found on Robert Pickton's pig farm. But it's the story of the victims that stays with me, not the killer.

True Crime Byline podcast: This Isn't a Story about Robert Pickton   Listen to this podcast episode for the heartbreaking history of Canada’s most prolific serial killer case, as told by Vancouver Sun reporter Lori Culbert.
'I’m just a driver': Canadian women set course for F1 glory   Could a woman, say, become a member of the most exclusive racing club in the world? Nicole Havrda says that is the plan
Spied! 2023 Ford Super Duty XLT swipes feature from Chevy   The truck was spied in prototype form being put to work in Detroit—but is that a GM-style integrated step?
Mercedes strikes second-life battery pact with Canadian startup   It will supply used batteries from the automaker’s EVs to Vancouver-based Moment Energy Inc.
Watch: 15-year-old flips Jeep off cliff, reacts like a boss   Okay, but our hearts were beating pretty fast before that “Somebody better have gotten that on video!”
GTA Lamborghini driver nabbed at nearly 3X the speed limit   “..we never get tired of yanking stunt drivers off our roads.”
TikTok Tuesday: #healthycoke — a trend that’s not so healthy for your teeth (WATCH)   It may be low in sugar, but carbonated water mixed with vinegar is a terrible choice for your teeth.
Putting our phones on silent mode only makes them more tempting, study says   Blame FOMO for how hard it can be for us to stop looking at our phones all the time.

Justin Bieber 'did something very important for the facial difference community'   Advocate Alim Somji says the pop star challenges the typical images of facial difference portrayed in the media as villains, such as Scar from the movie The Lion King.

WATCH: We take our medications out of the bathroom thanks to this TikTok trend   A bathrooms isn't a great storage option for meds, as the swings in temperatures and moist conditions can cause them to lose potency.
What are the signs of pancreatitis?   Acute and chronic pancreatitis are typically diagnosed between the ages of 40 and 70, with men more likely to be diagnosed across all age groups.

Man's ploy to flush over 20 kilograms of cannabis down the toilet leads him straight to jail   Officers also found 21 kilograms of weed, additional bags of prepackaged marijuana, a handgun and over $16K in cash.

Joe Rogan responds to border drug seizure: ‘You should take all these drugs and rethink your life’   Border agents seized small amounts of ecstasy, cannabis and psilocybin mushrooms from a vehicle entering New York.
Ontario casinos laundered $3 million in illegal cannabis proceeds: RCMP   Weed worth approx. $24.8 million confiscated.
One of Canada's biggest universities will have a pot store on campus   The planned store would represent the pot company Burb's eighth and final one allowed in the province under its retail cap.
Clint Eastwood handed $2.6 million after winning a second trademark infringement CBD lawsuit   Company did not have permission to use his image or likeness.
